results.title: Confidentiality Obligations
results._source_text_exact: 1.1 Each Receiving Party shall hold the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information in strict confidence and shall not disclose it to any third party without prior written consent, except to Representatives bound by confidentiality obligations at least as protective as this Agreement.
results.original_text: 1.1 Each Receiving Party shall hold the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information in strict confidence and shall not disclose it to any third party without prior written consent, except to Representatives bound by confidentiality obligations at least as protective as this Agreement.
results.reasoning_evidence: 1.1 Each Receiving Party shall hold the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information in strict confidence and shall not disclose it to any third party without pr
results.quoted_text: Each Receiving Party shall hold the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information in strict confidence and shall not disclose it to any third party without prior written consent, except to Representatives bound by confidentiality obligations at least as protective as this Agreement.
results.explanation_simple: The clause mandates confidentiality regarding the Disclosing Party's information.
results.why_it_matters: It protects sensitive information from unauthorized disclosure.
results.legal_insight: This clause establishes the core non-disclosure obligation: who is bound by it, what standard of care applies, and whether authorized sub-disclosure (e.g. to representatives) is permitted.
results.market_comparison: The clause aligns with common confidentiality practices in commercial agreements.
results.recommendation: Confirm the obligation clearly names who it binds, requires at least equivalent protection for any authorized sub-disclosure, and states a concrete standard of care.
section_path: 1. OBLIGATIONS OF CONFIDENTIALITY

1.1 Each Receiving Party shall hold the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information in strict confidence and shall not disclose it to any third party without prior written consent, except to Representatives bound by confidentiality obligations at least as protective as this Agreement.
risk_reasons: Confidentiality obligations may continue beyond contract termination.

results.title: Confidential Information Exclusions
results._source_text_exact: 2.1 Confidential Information does not include information that: (a) is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the Receiving Party; (b) was rightfully known to the Receiving Party prior to disclosure; (c) is independently developed without use of the Confidential Information; or (d) is rightfully received from a third party without duty of confidentiality.

results.title: Duration and Confidentiality Obligations
results._source_text_exact: 3.1 This Agreement shall remain in effect for two (2) years from the Effective Date. The confidentiality obligations herein shall survive for a period of five (5) years following disclosure of the applicable Confidential Information, except that obligations with respect to trade secrets shall survive for as long as such information remains a trade secret under applicable law.

section_path: 3. TERM AND SURVIVAL

3.1 This Agreement shall remain in effect for two (2) years from the Effective Date. The confidentiality obligations herein shall survive for a period of five (5) years following disclosure of the applicable Confidential Information, except that obligations with respect to trade secrets shall survive for as long as such information remains a trade secret under applicable law.

results.title: Confidential Information Acknowledgment
results._source_text_exact: 5.1 Each Party acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information may cause irreparable harm for which monetary damages would be an inadequate remedy, and that the non-breaching Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief in addition to any other available remedies, without the necessity of posting bond.
results.original_text: 5.1 Each Party acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information may cause irreparable harm for which monetary damages would be an inadequate remedy, and that the non-breaching Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief in addition to any other available remedies, without the necessity of posting bond.

results.explanation_simple: The clause allows for injunctive relief without bond for unauthorized disclosure of confidential information.
results.why_it_matters: This provision emphasizes the seriousness of unauthorized disclosures and allows for immediate legal action to prevent further harm.
results.market_comparison: This clause is relatively standard in confidentiality agreements, emphasizing the need for immediate remedies in case of breaches.

clauses.title: Confidentiality Obligations
clauses._source_text_exact: 1.1 Each Receiving Party shall hold the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information in strict confidence and shall not disclose it to any third party without prior written consent, except to Representatives bound by confidentiality obligations at least as protective as this Agreement.
clauses.original_text: 1.1 Each Receiving Party shall hold the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information in strict confidence and shall not disclose it to any third party without prior written consent, except to Representatives bound by confidentiality obligations at least as protective as this Agreement.
clauses.reasoning_evidence: 1.1 Each Receiving Party shall hold the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information in strict confidence and shall not disclose it to any third party without pr
clauses.quoted_text: Each Receiving Party shall hold the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information in strict confidence and shall not disclose it to any third party without prior written consent, except to Representatives bound by confidentiality obligations at least as protective as this Agreement.
clauses.explanation_simple: The clause mandates confidentiality regarding the Disclosing Party's information.
clauses.why_it_matters: It protects sensitive information from unauthorized disclosure.
clauses.legal_insight: This clause establishes the core non-disclosure obligation: who is bound by it, what standard of care applies, and whether authorized sub-disclosure (e.g. to representatives) is permitted.
clauses.market_comparison: The clause aligns with common confidentiality practices in commercial agreements.
clauses.recommendation: Confirm the obligation clearly names who it binds, requires at least equivalent protection for any authorized sub-disclosure, and states a concrete standard of care.
section_path: 1. OBLIGATIONS OF CONFIDENTIALITY

1.1 Each Receiving Party shall hold the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information in strict confidence and shall not disclose it to any third party without prior written consent, except to Representatives bound by confidentiality obligations at least as protective as this Agreement.
risk_reasons: Confidentiality obligations may continue beyond contract termination.

clauses.title: Use of Confidential Information
clauses._source_text_exact: 1.2 Each Receiving Party shall use the Confidential Information solely for the Purpose.

clauses.reasoning_evidence: 1.2 Each Receiving Party shall use the Confidential Information solely for the Purpose.
clauses.quoted_text: Each Receiving Party shall use the Confidential Information solely for the Purpose.
clauses.explanation_simple: The clause restricts the use of Confidential Information to a specific Purpose.
clauses.why_it_matters: This limitation helps protect sensitive information from being misused or disclosed.
clauses.legal_insight: This clause restricts what the receiving party may actually do with the confidential information, independent of whether it keeps it secret.
section_path: 1. OBLIGATIONS OF CONFIDENTIALITY
1.2 Each Receiving Party shall use the Confidential Information solely for the Purpose.

clauses.title: Confidential Information Exclusions
clauses._source_text_exact: 2.1 Confidential Information does not include information that: (a) is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the Receiving Party; (b) was rightfully known to the Receiving Party prior to disclosure; (c) is independently developed without use of the Confidential Information; or (d) is rightfully received from a third party without duty of confidentiality.
clauses.original_text: 2.1 Confidential Information does not include information that: (a) is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the Receiving Party; (b) was rightfully known to the Receiving Party prior to disclosure; (c) is independently developed without use of the Confidential Information; or (d) is rightfully received from a third party without duty of confidentiality.

clauses.recommendation: Confirm the exclusions are limited to the four standard carve-outs (public without breach, already known, independently developed, lawfully received from a third party) and are not broader than that.
section_path: 2. EXCLUSIONS

2.1 Confidential Information does not include information that: (a) is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the Receiving Party; (b) was rightfully known to the Receiving Party prior to disclosure; (c) is independently developed without use of the Confidential Information; or (d) is rightfully received from a third party without duty of confidentiality.
risk_reasons: Confidentiality obligations may continue beyond contract termination.

clauses.title: Duration and Confidentiality Obligations
clauses._source_text_exact: 3.1 This Agreement shall remain in effect for two (2) years from the Effective Date. The confidentiality obligations herein shall survive for a period of five (5) years following disclosure of the applicable Confidential Information, except that obligations with respect to trade secrets shall survive for as long as such information remains a trade secret under applicable law.
clauses.original_text: 3.1 This Agreement shall remain in effect for two (2) years from the Effective Date. The confidentiality obligations herein shall survive for a period of five (5) years following disclosure of the applicable Confidential Information, except that obligations with respect to trade secrets shall survive for as long as such information remains a trade secret under applicable law.
clauses.reasoning_evidence: 3.1 This Agreement shall remain in effect for two (2) years from the Effective Date. The confidentiality obligations herein shall survive for a period of five (
clauses.quoted_text: 3.1 This Agreement shall remain in effect for two (2) years from the Effective Date. The confidentiality obligations herein shall survive for a period of five (5) years following disclosure of the applicable Confidential Information, except that obligations with respect to trade secrets shall surviv
clauses.explanation_simple: The agreement has a defined duration and specifies the survival period for confidentiality obligations.
clauses.why_it_matters: Clearly defined timeframes help manage expectations regarding the duration of obligations and the protection of confidential information.
clauses.legal_insight: This clause determines how long the confidentiality obligations continue, including after the underlying agreement ends.
clauses.recommendation: Confirm the survival period is a defined term (commonly two to five years) rather than open-ended, except for a specific, narrower carve-out for genuine trade secrets.
section_path: 3. TERM AND SURVIVAL

3.1 This Agreement shall remain in effect for two (2) years from the Effective Date. The confidentiality obligations herein shall survive for a period of five (5) years following disclosure of the applicable Confidential Information, except that obligations with respect to trade secrets shall survive for as long as such information remains a trade secret under applicable law.
risk_reasons: Confidentiality obligations may continue beyond contract termination.
Compliance provisions may create ongoing legal, regulatory, or policy obligations.

clauses.title: Confidential Information Acknowledgment
clauses._source_text_exact: 5.1 Each Party acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information may cause irreparable harm for which monetary damages would be an inadequate remedy, and that the non-breaching Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief in addition to any other available remedies, without the necessity of posting bond.
clauses.original_text: 5.1 Each Party acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information may cause irreparable harm for which monetary damages would be an inadequate remedy, and that the non-breaching Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief in addition to any other available remedies, without the necessity of posting bond.
clauses.reasoning_evidence: 5.1 Each Party acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information may cause irreparable harm for which monetary damages would be an inadequat
clauses.quoted_text: Each Party acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information may cause irreparable harm for which monetary damages would be an inadequate remedy, and that the non-breaching Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief in addition to any other available remedies, without the necessity of posting bond.
clauses.explanation_simple: The clause allows for injunctive relief without bond for unauthorized disclosure of confidential information.
clauses.why_it_matters: This provision emphasizes the seriousness of unauthorized disclosures and allows for immediate legal action to prevent further harm.
clauses.market_comparison: This clause is relatively standard in confidentiality agreements, emphasizing the need for immediate remedies in case of breaches.
clauses.recommendation: Confirm the obligation clearly names who it binds, requires at least equivalent protection for any authorized sub-disclosure, and states a concrete standard of care.
section_path: 5. REMEDIES

5.1 Each Party acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information may cause irreparable harm for which monetary damages would be an inadequate remedy, and that the non-breaching Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief in addition to any other available remedies, without the necessity of posting bond.

clauses.title: Confidential Information Ownership

clauses.title: Governing Law and Jurisdiction
clauses._source_text_exact: 6.1 This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to conflict of laws principles. Any dispute arising under this Agreement shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state and federal courts located in Wilmington, Delaware.

section_path: 6. GOVERNING LAW AND DISPUTE RESOLUTION
